mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an emerging labor-market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as risk premia widen.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered UNRATE-rise episodes, or the effect disappears (t-stat < 1.5) when tested over a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal that consumer demand is expanding under low financing costs, a combination that historically precedes sustained equity rallies over a quarterly horizon; neither series alone captures this synergistic demand-liquidity effect.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 3% while FEDFUNDS below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth x FEDFUNDS deviation) shows no incremental predictive power over either single-series model in out-of-sample tests (delta Rank IC < 0.02), or joint-trigger episodes underperform the unconditional 60d return distribution.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates from prior highs, capacity utilization pressures ease and forward earnings growth slows, leading to below-average S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as markets price in a maturing cycle.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth rate falls below its trailing 24-month average
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following INDPRO deceleration signals are statistically indistinguishable from unconditional returns (p > 0.10) across at least two distinct late-cycle episodes in the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects improving labor income and consumer spending capacity, generating a short-lived positive equity reaction over the following trading week as markets incorporate the surprise into near-term growth expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month change remains below 0.02 Rank IC across rolling 1-year windows, indicating no persistent short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% month-over-month signals weakening residential investment demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as cyclical sectors reprice.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns following HOUST drop-triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising simultaneously with a falling unemployment rate, it indicates the Federal Reserve is tightening into a tightening labor market, raising the probability of policy overshoot and near-term equity volatility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week; the effect requires the joint occurrence of both conditions, as either alone is a normal expansionary signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months AND UNRATE falling over same window
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show 5d forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or single-series FEDFUNDS or UNRATE models achieve equal or better Rank IC than the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing four-quarter average signals diminishing economic momentum, leading markets to discount future earnings growth and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter; this relationship is expected to reverse in early-cycle recovery regimes where GDP deceleration reflects normalization rather than exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth falls below trailing 4-quarter average
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following GDPC1 deceleration are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) when conditioned on late-cycle regime classification, or the sign of the effect reverses inconsistently across at least two late-cycle episodes.
